title: M2 signing + --emit-certificate signed
manager: Milestone 2 of the trust stack — keyless signing lands at the latest here, so `--emit-certificate` produces a signed certificate rather than the bare record from v9. This is where a verification result becomes something you can hand to a third party as evidence: not just "I checked it" but a cryptographically signed claim bound to the exact source, verifiable by anyone who doesn't trust you and shouldn't have to.
cs: M2 keyless signing; `--emit-certificate` signed (upgrades the unsigned M1 record, W6.96); the signature layer over the certificate; may have attempted earlier in era 1, lands here at the latest; jssm twin #826.
